rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLENN R. WIGGINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the will was admitted on 5/24/18, and the creditor's claim period expiring on 9/25/18 is pursuant to Probate Code Section 9100(a), not the CCP. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]." [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVINDER SINGH SIDHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/24/21. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Although no courtesy copy has been received by the Examiner as of 1/25/21, a quick review of the case online indicates that the accounting begins with an amount that differs from the I&A. (The Judicial Council form GC-400 is for guardianships and conservatorships, not decedent's estates.) The decedent's death certificate shows the father as someone other than the father identified in these proceedings. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc., not just currently. (Only the bank statement verifying the ending balance needs to be filed in a decedent's estate.) If the administrator is the sole heir by virtue of the disclaimer, why is she accounting? A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, not just 100%. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/11/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MOJO TUBUTU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required in light of Merlin Williams, Jr.'s prior oral objection to petitioner's appointment as successor personal representative. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CURTIS C. CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/2021. 1) Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20] 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) The following people are mentioned in Decedent's Will and have not been listed on page 4 under #8 and have not received notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-121: a) the trustee of the curtis chancy chan 2019 trust dated april 3, 2018; b) Minh Nguyen; c) Jeffrey Alan Glickman. 4) DE-121 does not indicate the will is lost. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20] 5) Attachment 3(f)(3) does not address whether the will was last in the testator's possession and whether the testator was competent until death.
